title: succisa pratensis subsp. scotiaca
intro: Succisa pratensis subsp. scotiaca, also known as Devil's-bit scabious, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the borage family, native to Scotland and Ireland. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Devil's-bit scabious include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and even exhibit potential anticancer properties. The plant is typically prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is often used topically to treat skin irritations and promote healing.
